---
title: "Glossary & Index"
---

An alphabetical reference to the mechanisms in this guide and the other names they go by. A bold entry links to the article that covers it (the print edition adds its article number); an italic entry redirects an alias to its canonical term.


## #

*The 1:1*: see [One-on-One Scene Design](articles/players-social-dynamics/one-on-one-scene-design.qmd) [(76)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}


## A

**[A Taxonomy of Genre Conventions](articles/story-character-voice/a-taxonomy-of-genre-conventions.qmd)**: Map of genres by reader contract and key IF adaptation. [(48)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[A Taxonomy of Puzzles](articles/puzzles-clues-information/a-taxonomy-of-puzzles.qmd)**: Map of puzzle types: cognitive faculty × experiential job. [(22)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*A11y in Games*: see [Accessibility in Live & Interactive Games](articles/players-social-dynamics/accessibility-in-live-and-interactive-games.qmd) [(77)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Accessibility in Live & Interactive Games](articles/players-social-dynamics/accessibility-in-live-and-interactive-games.qmd)**: The social model; sensory/mobility/cognitive/linguistic access; hints as access. [(77)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Accessible Group Design*: see [Mixed-Ability Groups & Universal Design](articles/players-social-dynamics/mixed-ability-groups-and-universal-design.qmd) [(78)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Accusation Dynamics*: see [Trust, Conformity & Social Pressure](articles/players-social-dynamics/trust-conformity-and-social-pressure.qmd) [(61)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Action Economy*: see [Turn Structure & Action Economy](articles/systems-mechanics/turn-structure-and-action-economy.qmd) [(97)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Affection Systems*: see [Romance & Relationships](articles/story-character-voice/romance-and-relationships.qmd) [(44)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Age Banding*: see [Audience & Age-Targeting](articles/story-character-voice/audience-and-age-targeting.qmd) [(60)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Aha Moment](articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-aha-moment.qmd)**: The core cognitive reward of puzzle solving. [(27)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Alternate Reality Game (ARG)*: see [The Puzzle Hunt & ARG Tradition](articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-puzzle-hunt-and-arg-tradition.qmd) [(32)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Analysis Paralysis*: see [Turn Structure & Action Economy](articles/systems-mechanics/turn-structure-and-action-economy.qmd) [(97)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Answers as Data*: see [Extraction, Indexing & Meta-Puzzles](articles/puzzles-clues-information/extraction-indexing-and-meta-puzzles.qmd) [(25)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*ARG Lineage*: see [The Puzzle Hunt & ARG Tradition](articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-puzzle-hunt-and-arg-tradition.qmd) [(32)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Armchair Treasure Hunt*: see [Treasure & Scavenger Hunts](articles/puzzles-clues-information/treasure-and-scavenger-hunts.qmd) [(31)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Artefact Design & Document Production](articles/space-props-materiality/artefact-design-and-document-production.qmd)**: Designing and producing narrative objects (feelies, aged documents, dossiers). [(82)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Artefact Modality*: see [Physical vs Digital Puzzle Artefacts](articles/space-props-materiality/physical-vs-digital-puzzle-artefacts.qmd) [(91)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Asymmetric Win Conditions*: see [Faction Design & Asymmetric Objectives](articles/players-social-dynamics/faction-design-and-asymmetric-objectives.qmd) [(68)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Atmosphere*: see [Theme, Immersion & Sensory Environment](articles/space-props-materiality/theme-immersion-and-sensory-environment.qmd) [(81)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Audience & Age-Targeting](articles/story-character-voice/audience-and-age-targeting.qmd)**: Cross-genre age calibration (distinct from the Children's/YA genre craft). [(60)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Automa*: see [Solo Modes](articles/systems-mechanics/solo-modes.qmd) [(109)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}


## B

*Back-solving*: see Backsolving

**Backsolving**: Deriving a feeder answer from the meta rather than from the feeder itself.

*Baked-In Safety*: see [Safety Systems as Mechanical Layer](articles/systems-mechanics/safety-systems-as-mechanical-layer.qmd) [(103)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Beat Sheets*: see [Beat Taxonomies & Craft Models](articles/structure-pacing/beat-taxonomies-and-craft-models.qmd) [(7)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Beat Taxonomies & Craft Models](articles/structure-pacing/beat-taxonomies-and-craft-models.qmd)**: Save the Cat / Story Grid / Story Circle; mandatory vs branch-variable beats. [(7)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Bleed, Aftercare & Debriefing](articles/players-social-dynamics/bleed-aftercare-and-debriefing.qmd)**: Bleed (Emily Care Boss); de-roling, debrief types, bookend rituals. [(65)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Board Game Mechanisms*: see [Boardgame Mechanism Vocabulary](articles/systems-mechanics/boardgame-mechanism-vocabulary.qmd) [(114)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Boardgame Mechanism Vocabulary](articles/systems-mechanics/boardgame-mechanism-vocabulary.qmd)**: Map of boardgame mechanism families. Tabletop-only reference (Gate-C1). [(114)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Bot Deck*: see [Solo Modes](articles/systems-mechanics/solo-modes.qmd) [(109)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Bottleneck Mitigation*: see [Hint Systems & Recovery](articles/puzzles-clues-information/hint-systems-and-recovery.qmd) [(29)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Bottleneck Players*: see [Team Sizes & the Bottleneck Problem](articles/players-social-dynamics/team-sizes-and-the-bottleneck-problem.qmd) [(69)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Braided Narrative*: see [Parallel Threads & Convergence](articles/structure-pacing/parallel-threads-and-convergence.qmd) [(13)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Branch Budgeting*: see [Scope, Scale & Branching Depth](articles/structure-pacing/scope-scale-and-branching-depth.qmd) [(9)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Branch Voice Auditing*: see [Voice & Register Consistency](articles/story-character-voice/voice-and-register-consistency.qmd) [(36)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Branching Design*: see [Branching Narrative Craft](articles/structure-pacing/branching-narrative-craft.qmd) [(1)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Branching Narrative Craft](articles/structure-pacing/branching-narrative-craft.qmd)**: Choice architecture; meaningful vs illusory choice; convergence and state-tracking. [(1)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Branching Topologies*: see [IF Graph Structures: A Taxonomy](articles/structure-pacing/if-graph-structures-a-taxonomy.qmd) [(2)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Buy vs Make*: see [Fabrication & Sourcing](articles/space-props-materiality/fabrication-and-sourcing.qmd) [(83)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Diegetic Design](articles/story-character-voice/diegetic-design.qmd)**: The IF/narrative home of diegesis; show-the-world-hide-the-gears. (Live/physical sibling: Theme/Immersion, Family 5; boardgame sibling: Theme-Mechanism Integration, Family 6.) [(38)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Diegetic Devices*: see [Digital Props](articles/space-props-materiality/digital-props.qmd) [(87)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Difficulty Curve*: see [Session Pacing & Energy Management](articles/structure-pacing/session-pacing-and-energy-management.qmd) [(6)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Digital Gating & State](articles/systems-mechanics/digital-gating-and-state.qmd)**: Digital infrastructure for hunts/ARGs/hybrid rooms; the digital mechanism-as-judge. [(113)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Distinct Voices*: see [Character Voice](articles/story-character-voice/character-voice.qmd) [(33)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Dread Design*: see [Horror Conventions](articles/story-character-voice/horror-conventions.qmd) [(50)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Epiphany*: see [Aha Moment](articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-aha-moment.qmd) [(27)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Episodic & Serialised Structure](articles/structure-pacing/episodic-and-serialised-structure.qmd)**: Episodic vs serialised; state persistence across instalments; release cadence. [(14)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Episodic Design*: see [Episodic & Serialised Structure](articles/structure-pacing/episodic-and-serialised-structure.qmd) [(14)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**Epistemic Tier**: Analytical label for role/venue-differentiated knowledge in large live games (settled here as the canonical term; corpus flagged it as a label, not settled community usage).

*Escape Room Flow*: see [Escape Room Topologies](articles/structure-pacing/escape-room-topologies.qmd) [(10)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Escape Room Topologies](articles/structure-pacing/escape-room-topologies.qmd)**: Linear/open/multi-linear/hub-and-spoke rooms; why IF branching rarely survives a physical room. [(10)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Eureka*: see [Aha Moment](articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-aha-moment.qmd) [(27)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Euro vs Ameritrash: The Two Design Poles](articles/systems-mechanics/euro-vs-ameritrash-the-two-design-poles.qmd)**: The two boardgame design poles and the hybrid era. Tabletop-only reference (Gate-C1). [(115)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*The Euro-Ameritrash Axis*: see [Euro vs Ameritrash: The Two Design Poles](articles/systems-mechanics/euro-vs-ameritrash-the-two-design-poles.qmd) [(115)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Exposition & Subtext](articles/story-character-voice/exposition-and-subtext.qmd)**: Delivering information without a lecture; the unsaid. [(37)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Extraction, Indexing & Meta-Puzzles](articles/puzzles-clues-information/extraction-indexing-and-meta-puzzles.qmd)**: Answers-as-data; the meta and its extraction mechanics. [(25)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Fear Mechanics*: see [Horror Conventions](articles/story-character-voice/horror-conventions.qmd) [(50)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Feeder*: see Feeder Answer

**Feeder Answer**: A component-puzzle answer that feeds a meta.

*Fiat vs Ruling*: see [Rulings vs Rules & the Authority Contract](articles/players-social-dynamics/rulings-vs-rules-and-the-authority-contract.qmd) [(80)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Pre-Larp Workshop*: see [Pre-Game Workshop Design](articles/players-social-dynamics/pre-game-workshop-design.qmd) [(63)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Prep Economy](articles/systems-mechanics/prep-economy.qmd)**: Prepping the reusable; situational over plotted prep; the overprep failure. [(102)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Procedural Rhetoric*: see [IF Mechanics & Procedural Rhetoric](articles/systems-mechanics/if-mechanics-and-procedural-rhetoric.qmd) [(112)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Progress Bar*: see Progress Clock

**Progress Clock**: A visual widget for tracking state changes.

**[Progress Clocks & Faction State](articles/systems-mechanics/progress-clocks-and-faction-state.qmd)**: The home of the canonical **Progress Clock** (see that row); offscreen change and faction state. [(101)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Project Scoping*: see [Scope, Scale & Branching Depth](articles/structure-pacing/scope-scale-and-branching-depth.qmd) [(9)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Pure Branching*: see Time Cave

*Puzzlehunt Construction*: see [Puzzle Hunt Craft](articles/puzzles-clues-information/puzzle-hunt-craft.qmd) [(30)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**Puzzles, Clues & Information**: The cognitive loop of investigation and discovery.

**[Rulings vs Rules & the Authority Contract](articles/players-social-dynamics/rulings-vs-rules-and-the-authority-contract.qmd)**: Rulings-not-rules; Rule Zero and its abuse; authority held by table consent. [(80)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

## S

**Safety Contract**: The umbrella concept: the social agreement on content and boundaries. The specific tools (X-Card, Lines and Veils, Script Change, Lookdown, OK Check-In, Cut/Brake) are distinct named techniques, **not** aliases to swap. A safety article must name them precisely (see Safety Tools as Design Layer).

**[Safety Systems as Mechanical Layer](articles/systems-mechanics/safety-systems-as-mechanical-layer.qmd)**: Safety carried in the rules (Trophy dice, Strings); the systems-side complement to Safety Tools (Family 4). [(103)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Safety Tools as Design Layer](articles/players-social-dynamics/safety-tools-as-design-layer.qmd)**: The safety toolkit as a design layer (X-Card, Script Change, Lookdown, etc.); see the Safety Contract umbrella term. [(64)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Sandbox vs Railroad*: see [Sandbox, Situation & the Railroad Spectrum](articles/structure-pacing/sandbox-situation-and-the-railroad-spectrum.qmd) [(16)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Sandbox, Situation & the Railroad Spectrum](articles/structure-pacing/sandbox-situation-and-the-railroad-spectrum.qmd)**: The railroad-to-sandbox spectrum; prep situations not plots; the quantum ogre. [(16)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Scan-to-Unlock*: see [QR & NFC Patterns](articles/space-props-materiality/qr-and-nfc-patterns.qmd) [(88)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Scavenger Hunt*: see [Treasure & Scavenger Hunts](articles/puzzles-clues-information/treasure-and-scavenger-hunts.qmd) [(31)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Scene & Sequel in Interactive Fiction](articles/structure-pacing/scene-and-sequel-in-interactive-fiction.qmd)**: The scene/sequel micro-rhythm; the sequel as the reflection-and-choice beat. [(3)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Scene Breaks*: see [Scene Structure & Transitions](articles/structure-pacing/scene-structure-and-transitions.qmd) [(4)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Scene Craft*: see [Scene Structure & Transitions](articles/structure-pacing/scene-structure-and-transitions.qmd) [(4)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Scene Structure & Transitions](articles/structure-pacing/scene-structure-and-transitions.qmd)**: The beat-to-prose pipeline; cuts, transitions, scene breaks. [(4)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Scene-Sequel*: see [Scene & Sequel in Interactive Fiction](articles/structure-pacing/scene-and-sequel-in-interactive-fiction.qmd) [(3)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Science Fiction Conventions](articles/story-character-voice/science-fiction-conventions.qmd)**: The hard/soft contract; the novum; diegetic interfaces. [(52)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Scope Discipline*: see [Scope, Scale & Branching Depth](articles/structure-pacing/scope-scale-and-branching-depth.qmd) [(9)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Scope, Scale & Branching Depth](articles/structure-pacing/scope-scale-and-branching-depth.qmd)**: Estimating scale; how branching depth multiplies scope; cutting to finish. [(9)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*The Score*: see [Heist & Caper Patterns](articles/story-character-voice/heist-and-caper-patterns.qmd) [(43)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Search, Observation & Lateral Thinking](articles/puzzles-clues-information/search-observation-and-lateral-thinking.qmd)**: The noticing / perceptual faculty. [(23)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Secondary-World Craft*: see [Fantasy Conventions](articles/story-character-voice/fantasy-conventions.qmd) [(51)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Self-Resolving Puzzles](articles/puzzles-clues-information/self-resolving-puzzles.qmd)**: The apparatus confirms the solution itself. [(26)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Self-Validating Puzzles*: see [Self-Resolving Puzzles](articles/puzzles-clues-information/self-resolving-puzzles.qmd) [(26)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*The Three Pillars*: see [Encounter & Challenge Design](articles/systems-mechanics/encounter-and-challenge-design.qmd) [(99)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**Time Cave**: A narrative topology where every choice branches.

*Workshop Tiers*: see [Fabrication & Sourcing](articles/space-props-materiality/fabrication-and-sourcing.qmd) [(83)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*World-Affordance Puzzles*: see [Search, Observation & Lateral Thinking](articles/puzzles-clues-information/search-observation-and-lateral-thinking.qmd) [(23)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

**[Worldbuilding Patterns](articles/story-character-voice/worldbuilding-patterns.qmd)**: Internal logic, Sanderson's First Law, the iceberg, branching world-state. [(45)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Wretched & Alone Games*: see [Solo Journaling RPGs: Genre & Form](articles/story-character-voice/solo-journaling-rpgs-genre-and-form.qmd) [(56)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}

*Writing as the Game*: see [The Journal as Artefact](articles/story-character-voice/the-journal-as-artefact.qmd) [(58)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}


## Y

*Yes-But / No-And*: see [Try-Fail Cycles & Escalation](articles/structure-pacing/try-fail-cycles-and-escalation.qmd) [(5)]{.content-visible when-format="typst"}
